name: Pozas de Melon and Fervenza Touron - circular along the Cerves River
name_original: Pozas de Melón y Fervenza Tourón – circular por el río Cerves
region_intro: Hiking trails around Melón are characterized by a diverse landscape, featuring an expansive water body and shoreline, along with oak woodlands and foothill vegetation. The region offers a variety of trails that traverse areas with rivers, creeks, and unique geological formations. These paths provide opportunities to explore the natural environment, including scenic views of the surrounding terrain. Best hiking trails around Melón The most popular hiking route is Pozas de Melon and Fervenza Touron - circular along the Cerves River, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 48 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Cerves River, offering views of waterfalls and natural pools. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pozas de Melón – Tourón Waterfalls loop from Melón, an easy 2.8 miles (4.6 km) path. This loop provides access to the scenic Tourón Waterfalls and is often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Local hikers also love the Ribadavia Castle – Ribadavia Castle loop from A Arnoia, a 17.6 miles (28.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 57 minutes. This difficult route offers a longer exploration of the wider area. Hiking around Melón is defined by river valleys, waterfalls, and diverse woodlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging, longer hikes. The routes in Melón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Melón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Melón?
A: There are 10 hiking trails around Melón, offering a variety of experiences. Six of these are classified as easy, two as moderate, and two as difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there any trails with waterfalls in Melón?
A: Yes, Melón is known for its beautiful waterfalls and natural pools. You can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976213063/pozas-de-melon-and-fervenza-touron-circular-along-the-cerves-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melon and Fervenza Touron - circular along the Cerves River&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Cerves River and offers views of waterfalls. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón – Tourón Waterfalls loop from Melón&lt;/a&gt;, providing access to the scenic Tourón Waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268566" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourón Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are notable highlights in the area.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Melón?
A: Hiking around Melón offers diverse landscapes, characterized by river valleys, expansive water bodies, and lush woodlands. You'll encounter oak woodlands, foothill vegetation, and unique geological formations. Many trails provide scenic views of the surrounding terrain and access to rivers and creeks.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Melón?
A: Absolutely. Melón offers several easy trails suitable for families. Six of the ten available routes are classified as easy, making them ideal for a leisurely stroll with children. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24309347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tourón Waterfalls – Fervenza do Tourón loop from Melón&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, easy option at 2.3 km, perfect for a family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Melón?
A: While many outdoor areas in the region are dog-friendly, specific regulations for each trail can vary. It's always best to check local signage at trailheads or official park websites for the most current rules regarding dogs, especially concerning leash requirements and waste disposal.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Melón?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976213063/pozas-de-melon-and-fervenza-touron-circular-along-the-cerves-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melon and Fervenza Touron - circular along the Cerves River&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 6.1 km loop. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14935664" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón – Tourón Waterfalls loop from Melón&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 4.6 km path that takes you to the scenic Tourón Waterfalls.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks I can visit near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore several interesting attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6067935" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mestas Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are stunning natural features. For historical interest, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7862421" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadavia Castle&lt;/a&gt; is a significant landmark. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130126" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castrelo de Miño Nautical Park&lt;/a&gt; for lake activities or see the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5014761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Esperela&lt;/a&gt; bridge.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Melón?
A: The region, often referred to as New Melones Lake in California's Gold Country, is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and fall. Spring brings vibrant wildflowers and moderate temperatures, while fall offers cooler weather and beautiful foliage. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, especially if seeking out spots like caves for cooling off.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Melón?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more strenuous adventure, Melón offers two difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadavia Castle – Ribadavia Castle loop from A Arnoia&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 28.4 km trail that takes nearly 8 hours to complete, leading through varied terrain and offering a longer exploration of the wider area.

Q: Where can I find natural pools or wild swimming spots along the trails?
A: The area is well-known for its natural pools, locally called 'pozas,' and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3006372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melón&lt;/a&gt; are a prime example, offering refreshing spots. Trails like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976213063/pozas-de-melon-and-fervenza-touron-circular-along-the-cerves-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Melon and Fervenza Touron - circular along the Cerves River&lt;/a&gt; often lead directly to these scenic water features.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Melón?
A: The hiking trails in Melón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the scenic river valleys, beautiful waterfalls, and diverse woodlands, highlighting the variety of options available for different ability levels.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Melón?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, major recreation areas like Glory Hole and Tuttletown (referring to the broader New Melones Lake region) typically serve as hubs for outdoor activities and offer designated parking facilities for trail access. It's advisable to check specific route information for detailed parking instructions.

Q: Can I reach the hiking trails in Melón using public transport?
A: Information regarding public transport access directly to the trailheads in Melón is not readily available. Most visitors to this region typically rely on personal vehicles to reach the various hiking starting points.
tips: Las Pozas de Melón are a natural enclave of the Cerves river that are located in the southwest of the Galician province of Ourense and belong to the municipality of Melón.

In the pools located in the Municipality of Melón, a tributary of the Miño, there are some impressive waterfalls with a drop of about 120 metres. In the middle part of the Cascadas do Tourón, there is a wooden and metal bridge that allows you to cross from one side of the river to the other.
